ranking.option: Option C: Engine -> Service -> Multiple Runtime Adapters
ranking.justification: Provides maximum extensibility (HTTP, Cron, Queue) while decoupling the engine from delivery specifics. Smallest additive change, maps cleanly to existing service pattern.

ranking.option: Option B: Engine -> Service -> Express Route
ranking.justification: Good isolation, but artificially limits execution to HTTP routes.

ranking.option: Option D: Engine -> Dependency Injection Container -> Runtime Adapters
ranking.justification: Requires a massive rewrite of the Express architecture to introduce a DI framework (like InversifyJS or NestJS), violating 'smallest additive implementation'.
Couples business logic directly to Express.Req/Res objects. Destroys language neutrality and prevents background execution.
